title: Little Mary's Linnet. (From The Villager's Verse-Book.)
content:
Dear Mary, if thy little bird
    Should, all the winter long,
    Pleased from the window to be heard,
    Repay thee with a song;

     A lesson let it still convey
    To all with sense endued;
    And such the voice, oh! let it say,
    The still small voice of love.
meaning:
This evocative piece by William Lisle Bowles, titled "Little Mary's Linnet. (From The Villager's Verse-Book.)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
